message.content:
Mechanical line-relocation in each file below. Each currently has a line `source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buym_yelp.sh"` INDENTED inside a furnish function (e.g. `zXXX_furnish()`). It must move to the file HEADER.

EXACT transformation per file:
1. DELETE the existing line that reads (with its leading whitespace):  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buym_yelp.sh"
   — the one inside the furnish function. Remove the whole line.
2. INSERT a new line `source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buym_yelp.sh"` (NO leading whitespace) immediately AFTER the header line that reads exactly:  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
   (that buc_command line is near the top of the file, at file scope / not inside any function).

Net effect: the buym_yelp.sh source moves from inside furnish to the header, directly below the buc_command.sh source. Change nothing else. Every one of these files uses ${BURD_BUK_DIR} for both lines, so the variable is unchanged.

Verify after editing each file: `grep -n 'buc_command.sh\|buym_yelp.sh' <file>` should show the two on consecutive lines near the top, and NO buym_yelp.sh line remaining inside the furnish function.

FILES:
- Tools/rbk/rbrp_cli.sh
- Tools/rbk/rbfc_cli.sh
- Tools/rbk/rbfv_cli.sh
- Tools/rbk/rbro_cli.sh
- Tools/rbk/rbra_cli.sh
- Tools/rbk/rbgg_cli.sh
- Tools/rbk/rbfk_cli.sh
- Tools/rbk/rbrs_cli.sh
- Tools/rbk/rbtd/rbte_cli.sh
- Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/rbv_cli.sh
- Tools/jjk/jjfp_cli.sh
- Tools/vok/vob_cli.sh

REPORT: one line per file: `<file>: MOVED` or `<file>: PROBLEM - <description>`. Do not run tests or git commands.

attachment.content:
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the current diff and apply the fixes — equivalent to /code-review --fix.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll read all files first to understand their structure, then make the edits.

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# RBRP CLI - Command line interface for RBRP payor operations
20	
21	set -euo pipefail
22	
23	source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
24	
25	######################################################################
26	# Command Functions
27	
28	rbrp_validate() {
29	  buc_doc_brief "Validate RBRP payor regime configuration via enrollment report"
30	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
31	
32	  buc_step "Validating RBRP payor file: ${RBCC_rbrp_file}"
33	  buv_report RBRP "Payor Regime"
34	  buc_step "RBRP payor valid"
35	}
36	
37	rbrp_render() {
38	  buc_doc_brief "Display diagnostic view of RBRP payor regime configuration"
39	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
40	
41	  buv_render RBRP "RBRP - Recipe Bottle Regime Payor" "${RBCC_rbrp_file}"
42	}
43	
44	######################################################################
45	# Furnish and Main
46	
47	zrbrp_furnish() {
48	  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
49	  buc_doc_env_done || return 0
50	
51	  local z_rbk_kit_dir="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
52	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
53	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
54	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bupr_PresentationRegime.sh"
55	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buym_yelp.sh"
56	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbcc_Constants.sh"
57	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgc_Constants.sh"
58	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrp_regime.sh"
59	
60	  zbuv_kindle
61	  zburd_kindle
62	  zburd_enforce
63	  zrbcc_kindle
64	  zrbgc_kindle
65	
66	  source "${RBCC_rbrp_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source RBRP: ${RBCC_rbrp_file}"
67	
68	  zrbrp_kindle
69	  zrbrp_enforce
70	
71	  zbupr_kindle
72	}
73	
74	buc_execute rbrp_ "Recipe Bottle Payor Regime" zrbrp_furnish "$@"
75	
76	# eof
77

50	
51	  zrbcc_kindle
52	
53	  zrbrr_kindle
54	  zrbrd_kindle
55	  zrbrr_enforce
56	  zrbrd_enforce
57	  zrbdc_kindle
58	
59	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrv_regime.sh"
60	
61	  buc_log_args 'Kindle modules in dependency order'
62	  zrbgc_kindle
63	  zrbgl_kindle
64	  zrbgd_kindle
65	  zrbgo_kindle
66	  zrbfc_kindle
67	}
68	
69	buc_execute rbfc_ "Recipe Bottle Foundry Core" zrbfc_furnish "$@"

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# RBRO CLI - Command line interface for RBRO OAuth operations
20	
21	set -euo pipefail
22	
23	source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
24	
25	######################################################################
26	# Command Functions
27	
28	# Command: validate - enrollment-based validation (dies on first error)
29	# SECURITY: buv_report shows raw values — use enforce + manual report for secrets
30	rbro_validate() {
31	  buc_doc_brief "Validate RBRO OAuth credential file via enrollment enforcement"
32	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
33	
34	  buc_step "Validating RBRO OAuth credential file: ${RBDC_PAYOR_RBRO_FILE}"
35	  buc_step "  PASS  RBRO_CLIENT_SECRET=[REDACTED - ${#RBRO_CLIENT_SECRET} chars] [string]"
36	  buc_step "  PASS  RBRO_REFRESH_TOKEN=[REDACTED - ${#RBRO_REFRESH_TOKEN} chars] [string]"
37	  buc_step "RBRO OAuth credential file valid"
38	}
39	
40	# Command: render - diagnostic display with secret masking
41	# SECURITY: Temporarily masks secret values before calling buv_render
42	rbro_render() {
43	  buc_doc_brief "Display diagnostic view of RBRO OAuth regime configuration"
44	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
45	
46	  echo ""
47	  echo "${BUC_white}RBRO - Recipe Bottle OAuth Regime${BUC_reset}"
48	  echo ""
49	  buc_step "  RBRO_CLIENT_SECRET=[REDACTED - ${#RBRO_CLIENT_SECRET} chars] [string]"
50	  buc_step "  RBRO_REFRESH_TOKEN=[REDACTED - ${#RBRO_REFRESH_TOKEN} chars] [string]"
51	}
52	
53	######################################################################
54	# Furnish and Main
55	
56	zrbro_furnish() {
57	  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
58	  buc_doc_env_done || return 0
59	
60	  local z_rbk_kit_dir="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
61	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
62	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
63	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bupr_PresentationRegime.sh"
64	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buym_yelp.sh"
65	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbcc_Constants.sh"
66	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgc_Constants.sh"
67	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrr_regime.sh"
68	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrd_regime.sh"
69	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh"
70	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbro_regime.sh"
71	
72	  zbuv_kindle
73	  zburd_kindle
74	  zburd_enforce
75	  zrbcc_kindle
76	
77	  source "${RBCC_rbrr_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source RBRR: ${RBCC_rbrr_file}"
78	  source "${RBCC_rbrd_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source RBRD: ${RBCC_rbrd_file}"
79	  zrbrr_kindle
80	  zrbrd_kindle
81	  zrbrr_enforce
82	  zrbrd_enforce
83	  zrbdc_kindle
84	
85	  rbro_load
86	
87	  zbupr_kindle
88	}
89	
90	buc_execute rbro_ "Recipe Bottle OAuth Regime" zrbro_furnish "$@"
91	
92	# eof
93

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# RBRA CLI - Command line interface for RBRA credential operations
20	#
21	# Manifold regime: BUZ_FOLIO carries role name (governor, retriever, director).
22	# Role resolves to RBRA file path via RBRR references.
23	
24	set -euo pipefail
25	
26	source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
27	
28	######################################################################
29	# Internal Helpers
30	
31	# Resolve role name to RBRA file path via RBRR
32	zrbra_resolve_role() {
33	  local z_role="${1:-}"
34	  test -n "${z_role}" || buc_die "RBRA role required (governor|retriever|director)"
35	
36	  zrbrr_sentinel
37	
38	  case "${z_role}" in
39	    governor)  echo "${RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE}" ;;
40	    retriever) echo "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}" ;;
41	    director)  echo "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" ;;
42	    *)         buc_die "Unknown RBRA role: ${z_role}. Valid roles: governor, retriever, director" ;;
43	  esac
44	}
45	
46	######################################################################
47	# Command Functions
48	
49	rbra_validate() {
50	  buc_doc_brief "Validate RBRA credential regime configuration via enrollment report"
51	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
52	
53	  if test -z "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"; then
54	    rbra_list
55	    buc_die "RBRA role required"
56	  fi
57	  buc_step "Validating RBRA credential regime"
58	  buv_report RBRA "Credential Regime"
59	  buc_step "RBRA credential valid"
60	}
61	
62	rbra_render() {
63	  buc_doc_brief "Display diagnostic view of RBRA credential regime configuration"
64	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
65	
66	  if test -z "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"; then
67	    rbra_list
68	    buc_die "RBRA role required"
69	  fi
70	  local z_rbra_file
71	  z_rbra_file=$(zrbra_resolve_role "${BUZ_FOLIO}")
72	  buv_render RBRA "RBRA - Recipe Bottle Authentication Regime" "${z_rbra_file}"
73	}
74	
75	rbra_list() {
76	  buc_doc_brief "List RBRA credential roles and file paths"
77	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
78	
79	  zrbrr_sentinel
80	
81	  buc_step "RBRA credential roles (from RBRR):"
82	  local z_roles=("governor" "retriever" "director")
83	  local z_vars=("RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE" "RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE" "RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE")
84	
85	  local z_i
86	  for z_i in "${!z_roles[@]}"; do
87	    local z_role="${z_roles[$z_i]}"
88	    local z_path="${!z_vars[$z_i]:-}"
89	    local z_status="missing"
90	    test -f "${z_path}" && z_status="ok"
91	    buc_step "  ${z_role} [${z_status}] ${z_path}"
92	  done
93	}
94	
95	######################################################################
96	# Furnish and Main
97	
98	zrbra_furnish() {
99	  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
100	  buc_doc_env_done || return 0
101	
102	  local z_command="${1:-}"
103	
104	  # Light sources (always)
105	  local z_rbk_kit_dir="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
106	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
107	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
108	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bupr_PresentationRegime.sh"
109	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buym_yelp.sh"
110	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbcc_Constants.sh"
111	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgc_Constants.sh"
112	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrr_regime.sh"
113	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrd_regime.sh"
114	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh"
115	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbra_regime.sh"
116	
117	  # Light kindles (always)
118	  zbuv_kindle
119	  zburd_kindle
120	  zburd_enforce
121	  zbupr_kindle
122	  zrbcc_kindle
123	
124	  # Load RBRR (needed for role resolution and list)
125	  source "${RBCC_rbrr_file}"
126	  source "${RBCC_rbrd_file}"
127	  zrbrr_kindle
128	  zrbrd_kindle
129	  zrbrr_enforce
130	  zrbrd_enforce
131	  zrbdc_kindle
132	
133	  # If BUZ_FOLIO is set, load and kindle the specified role
134	  if test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"; then
135	    local z_rbra_file
136	    z_rbra_file=$(zrbra_resolve_role "${BUZ_FOLIO}")
137	    test -f "${z_rbra_file}" || buc_die "RBRA file not found for role ${BUZ_FOLIO}: ${z_rbra_file}"
138	    source "${z_rbra_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source RBRA: ${z_rbra_file}"
139	    zrbra_kindle
140	    zrbra_enforce
141	
142	    # Swizzle guard: RBRA_ROLE must match the expected role from folio
143	    test "${RBRA_ROLE}" = "${BUZ_FOLIO}" \
144	      || buc_die "RBRA_ROLE swizzle: file declares role '${RBRA_ROLE}' but loaded as '${BUZ_FOLIO}'"
145	  fi
146	}
147	
148	buc_execute rbra_ "Recipe Bottle Authentication Regime" zrbra_furnish "$@"
149	
150	# eof

37	rbrs_render() {

50

52	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
53	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"

56	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbcc_Constants.sh"
57	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrs_regime.sh"
58	
59	  zbuv_kindle
60	  zburd_kindle
61	  zburd_enforce
62	  zrbcc_kindle
63	
64	  source "${RBCC_rbrs_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source RBRS: ${RBCC_rbrs_file}"
65	
66	  zrbrs_kindle
67	  zrbrs_enforce
68	
69	  zbupr_kindle
70	}
71	
72	buc_execute rbrs_ "Recipe Bottle Station Regime" zrbrs_furnish "$@"

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
3	#
4	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
5	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
6	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
7	#
8	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
9	#
10	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
11	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
12	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
13	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
14	# limitations under the License.
15	#
16	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
17	#
18	# Recipe Bottle VM - Command Line Interface
19	
20	set -euo pipefail
21	
22	source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
23	
24	# CLI-specific environment function
25	zrbv_furnish() {
26	  # Handle documentation mode
27	  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
28	  buc_doc_env "RBV_TEMP_DIR          " "Empty temporary directory"
29	  buc_doc_env "RBV_RBRR_FILE         " "File containing the RBRR constants"
30	  buc_doc_env "RBV_RBRS_FILE         " "File containing the RBRS constants"
31	  buc_doc_env_done || return 0
32	
33	  local z_rbk_kit_dir="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/.."
34	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
35	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buym_yelp.sh"
36	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrr_regime.sh"
37	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrd_regime.sh"
38	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrm_regime.sh"
39	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh"
40	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrs_regime.sh"
41	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbv_PodmanVM.sh"
42	
43	  # Initialize enrollment and validate environment
44	  zbuv_kindle
45	  buv_dir_exists  "${RBV_TEMP_DIR}"
46	  buv_dir_empty   "${RBV_TEMP_DIR}"
47	  buv_file_exists "${RBV_RBRR_FILE}"
48	  buv_file_exists "${RBV_RBRS_FILE}"
49	
50	  # Source config files (CLI handles all sourcing)
51	  source              "${RBV_RBRR_FILE}"   || buc_die "Failed to source RBRR config"
52	  source              "${RBCC_rbrd_file}"  || buc_die "Failed to source RBRD config"
53	  source              "${RBCC_rbrm_file}"  || buc_die "Failed to source RBRM config"
54	  zrbrr_kindle
55	  zrbrd_kindle
56	  zrbrr_enforce
57	  zrbrd_enforce
58	  zrbrm_kindle
59	  zrbrm_enforce
60	  zrbdc_kindle
61	
62	  source              "${RBV_RBRS_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source RBRS config"
63	  zrbrs_kindle
64	  zrbrs_enforce
65	
66	  # Start implementation module
67	  zrbv_kindle
68	}
69	
70	# Execute command
71	buc_execute rbv_ "Recipe Bottle VM - Podman Virtual Machine Management" zrbv_furnish "$@"
72	
73	# eof
